content.question: What is the causal effect of Hungary's early selection on mean student performance (as opposed to inequality)? Cross-national studies remain contested on this dimension.
content.reduction_path: Commission a Hungary-specific longitudinal or quasi-experimental study (e.g., exploiting natural variation in school-entry cohort exposure to track-eligibility cutoffs, or comparing similar students just above/below selection thresholds within the same districts) to isolate tracking effect on average outcomes, separate from selection effects
